data.narrative:
Interlune is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Rob Meyerson, Gary Lai, and Harrison Schmitt that has raised $23M. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though no narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier exists within the Space & Aerospace sector.

Two standard triangulation methods cannot be applied. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable because no subset of 3+ same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with similar valuation tiers exists—while 11 same-sector peers are cataloged, the filtering criteria yield insufficient data points. Public-comp anchoring is similarly blocked: the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S multiple stands at 25.8x across 6 public tickers, but Interlune's revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies at this stage and prevents any revenue-based valuation bridge.

No triangulated valuation range can be supported by the available data. Meaningful analysis would require either disclosure of Interlune's revenue (enabling public-multiple anchoring), identification of 3+ truly comparable seed-stage Space & Aerospace peers with disclosed valuations, or alternative methods such as forward-revenue projections or venture capital financing-round benchmarking. The primary uncertainty is the absence of quantitative operational metrics; downstream analysis must wait for additional disclosure or comparable transaction data.
